item.title: I love my in-laws.
item.description: https://imgur.com/gallery/Bt3SDYN Edit: I felt embarrassed because when I opened up to my own mother about it, she scoffed at me, rolled her eyes, said there's nothing wrong with me and that my psychiatrist is just treating me like a guinea pig. She said my mind isn't strong enough because I don't eat healthy enough. I eat super healthy. I felt like shit. Made me not want to let my husband's family know.
item.dc:creator: Zinfandella

item.title: Images of Note: I've somehow angered the government
item.description:
Love how the limp wrist is a good way to ask if someone is queer by subtly.
HELL YEAH
remember this
PINGU NO
:3
AAAAAAAAAAA
that's not hard to do.
There's nobody more cruel than the "Chets"
:)
f r o g s
It's almost like forcing people to rent everything is more profitable for capitalists than letting you own something.
love how you can now basically say magic words to bots on twitter now and reprogram them.
uwu
http://discord.abbii.gay: For Chats http://twitch.abbii.gay: For Stream (m/w/f around 1830 UK time) http://tumblr.abbii.gay: For shitposts http://mastodon.abbii.gay: For microblogging

that's not motor oil!
pretty terrible coloring job by all
classy
is that normal?
much more
rod stewart is the man
vegan cocaine slaps
mmm hmm
i guess
it does really work
i don't know what any of this means
it should be
an odd read
gross
yes i've posted this at least 8 times
what of it?
that's my time.. have a great night gang!!

item.dc:creator: Herebychoice

item.title: Fuck you Netflix!
item.description: Rant time! Okay, I understand that tech companies cannot keep their services compatible with all (older) hardware devices out there for ever. But the arrogant tone and blatant lie in this email rubbed me the wrong way. Netflix: you cannot use your device anymore to watch our shows and films. But please understand that we do this to so you can maintain the best possible viewing experience. Huh? What?? No!! The best possible viewing experience would be if I can keep using the tv I have always used to watch Netflix on. It always worked, why would you change it so that it doesn’t work anymore, and then claim that it will improve my viewing experience?? Get the fuck out of here with that bullshit. Time to find my old buccaneer hat, and set sail for the Bay! Ok. Rant over. Thanks for listening Imgur. I needed to get that out of my system.
item.dc:creator: BigBlueWonder

item.dc:creator: dickwitfo

item.title: I was in St. Louis and ended up taking probably my favorite picture of The Arch.
item.description: Edit: Holy cow! Front page? I'm going to have to have this picture framed. Thanks for the kind words! Some context - The blue light on the trees are from some police cars parked off to the side (there was a mild fender bender or something and they had that lane blocked off). I was walking back to my hotel after pigging out and thought it was a neat view.

item.title: puppies :)
item.dc:creator: acuteavocado2

item.title: I'm cancer free!
item.description: Back in April, I was diagnosed with colon cancer. A mass was detected in my lower sigmoid colon, and after going through numerous tests and scans, it was determined that I needed surgery. Last Friday, doctors removed about a foot of my colon, and what pathology revealed to have been a stage 2 tumor. No other organs were affected. And as per my post-op meeting this morning, I will not be needing chemotherapy or radiation treatments, just occasional checkups. I was lucky. My advice to you: GET TESTED. I was experiencing zero symptoms. There was no pain, no discomfort, no blood, and no family history. I had no idea anything was abnormal. I only went because I’m over 50, and that’s what you’re supposed to do. Had I waited much longer, I would have had a very different and more grim prognosis. In fact, had I been tested at 40, or 45, as they now recommend, it would have been limited to an outpatient procedure.
item.dc:creator: Mindcrime

item.title: Last Wrongs?
item.description: I’m a loner (Dottie!) but not much of a rebel. Shortness of breath, edema, a CT scan showed fluid and two likely tumors in my lungs (most likely metastasis from stage iv colon cancer - that had otherwise been wiped out since 2018!). I feel sicker day by day. But it is what it is. I still have a bunch of tests and scans in the next week. I’ve got a great job but I’m not close to anyone there. I’m a little worried that if I come forward with this info now, well, it just seems like a hassle. I still need the job to pay rent and all, and I’d worry about them doing something that might screw my (estranged) family out of a fairly large work provided life insurance. (US / California rules would apply) Other than clearing my browser history (oof, so many old computers!) has anyone ever had to deal with an employee informing them of their sell-by-date? Or should I just go ahead and die when I need to and let them deal with it then? Or, should I leave a tantalizing trail of clues to a fabulous prize, that I never finish but it’s ok because the real prize was solving the clues? #health #sad #dark_humor
item.dc:creator: OopsIDwintAgain

item.title: 96 pounds down!
item.description: Done the old fashioned way, tracking calories, calorie deficit and weight lifting + light cardio are all it took! Plus the mindset and believing that you can do it!
item.dc:creator: Tfay2100

item.title: I stole this dump, so you don't have to. Tuesday random meme dump of pure chaos
item.description:
1. The age of men is over, the time of chicken has come!
2. He's been hurt too many times, got it right this time
3. Movie extra records a kid practicing lines
4. A truly unhackable door
5. The wingman we all need
6
7
8. You can take only one on a date - left or right?
9
10. Alien Animal Planet: "Here we have a female of homo species doing a traditional mating dance. Let's see if it works!"
11. Dog helps settle a dispute
12
13. Cleaning up the streets
14. No need for a paternity test over here
15. Thank you for making it to the end of the dump. I hope you don't mind me sharing that for the past year and a half, I’ve been working on a game that’s very close to my heart. "Do Not Press The Button (To Delete The Multiverse)" is a quirky adventure crafted with love and passion, with my brother by my side, helping every step of the way. As indie developers, your support means the world to us. By adding our game to your wishlist, you’ll help us reach more players and make a big impact in the gaming community. Here it is: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ We can’t do this without you. Thank you for supporting our dream and joining us on this wild adventure!

item.title: Story Time
item.description: The car in question was sold 8 years ago, my friend doesn’t play softball, I’m properly insured, and I haven’t been socially inept since high school. Jfc that sucked though. MV edit: Y’all are just the best. Be nice to someone today. <3
